1Ôćĺpackage api
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺimport (
     4Ôćĺ	"net/http"
     5Ôćĺ	"strings"
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8Ôćĺ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/auth"
     9Ôćĺ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    10Ôćĺ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    11Ôćĺ)
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    14Ôćĺtype Dependencies struct {
    15Ôćĺ	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    16Ôćĺ	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    17Ôćĺ	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    18Ôćĺ	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    19Ôćĺ	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    20Ôćĺ	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    21Ôćĺ	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    22Ôćĺ	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    23Ôćĺ	AgentStore            AgentStore
    24Ôćĺ	TodosStore            TodosStore
    25Ôćĺ	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
    26Ôćĺ	PublicURL             string
    27Ôćĺ	KeyStore              KeyStore
    28Ôćĺ	SessionValidator      SessionValidator
    29Ôćĺ	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    30Ôćĺ	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    31Ôćĺ	NoAuth                bool
    32Ôćĺ	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    33Ôćĺ	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    34Ôćĺ}
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    37Ôćĺfunc RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    38Ôćĺ	// Public chain: no auth required.
    39Ôćĺ	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    42Ôćĺ	chain := publicChain
    43Ôćĺ	if !deps.NoAuth {
    44Ôćĺ		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore, deps.SessionValidator))
    45Ôćĺ	}
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ	// Projects (CRUD)
    48Ôćĺ	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    49Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    50Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    51Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    52Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    53Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ	// Sources (nested under projects)
    56Ôćĺ	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    57Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    58Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    59Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    60Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    61Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    62Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    65Ôćĺ	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    66Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    67Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    68Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    69Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    70Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    73Ôćĺ	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    74Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    75Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    76Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    77Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    80Ôćĺ	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    81Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    82Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    83Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    84Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    85Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    88Ôćĺ	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    89Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    90Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    91Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    92Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    93Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    94Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    95Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    98Ôćĺ	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
    99Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
   100Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
   101Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
   102Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
   103Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
   104Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ	// Agent
   107Ôćĺ	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   108Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   109Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   110Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ	// Todos
   113Ôćĺ	todos := NewTodosHandler(deps.TodosStore, deps.PublicURL)
   114Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.List)))
   115Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Get)))
   116Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   117Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   118Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/reopen", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Reopen)))
   119Ôćĺ	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)
   120Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   121Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ	// Onboard (repo dependency scanning)
   124Ôćĺ	ob := NewOnboardHandler(deps.OnboardStore)
   125Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scan", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Scan)))
   126Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.GetScan)))
   127Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Apply)))
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ	// Providers (metadata ÔÇö static, no store needed)
   130Ôćĺ	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   131Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ	// Discovery (public ÔÇö no auth, proxies external APIs)
   134Ôćĺ	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   135Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   136Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ	// SSE events
   139Ôćĺ	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   140Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ	// Health (public ÔÇö no auth middleware)
   143Ôćĺ	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   144Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   145Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   146Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   147Ôćĺ}
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ// RegisterAuthRoutes registers OAuth and session endpoints on the mux.
   150Ôćĺ// frontendURL is used in noAuth mode to redirect back to the frontend (may be empty).
   151Ôćĺfunc RegisterAuthRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, oauthHandler *auth.OAuthHandler, noAuth bool, frontendURL string) {
   152Ôćĺ	if noAuth {
   153Ôćĺ		mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/me", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   154Ôćĺ			if c, err := r.Cookie("dev_logged_out"); err == nil && c.Value == "1" {
   155Ôćĺ				http.Error(w, `{"error":"unauthorized"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
   156Ôćĺ				return
   157Ôćĺ			}
   158Ôćĺ			auth.HandleMeDev(w, r)
   159Ôćĺ		})
   160Ôćĺ		mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/logout", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   161Ôćĺ			http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
   162Ôćĺ				Name:  "dev_logged_out",
   163Ôćĺ				Value: "1",
   164Ôćĺ				Path:  "/",
   165Ôćĺ			})
   166Ôćĺ			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   167Ôćĺ		})
   168Ôćĺ		mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   169Ôćĺ			// Clear the logout cookie so dev user is "logged in" again
   170Ôćĺ			http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
   171Ôćĺ				Name:   "dev_logged_out",
   172Ôćĺ				Value:  "",
   173Ôćĺ				Path:   "/",
   174Ôćĺ				MaxAge: -1,
   175Ôćĺ			})
   176Ôćĺ			target := "/"
   177Ôćĺ			if frontendURL != "" {
   178Ôćĺ				target = strings.TrimRight(frontendURL, "/") + "/"
   179Ôćĺ			}
   180Ôćĺ			http.Redirect(w, r, target, http.StatusFound)
   181Ôćĺ		})
   182Ôćĺ		return
   183Ôćĺ	}
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ	sessionMw := oauthHandler.RequireSession
   186Ôćĺ	mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github", oauthHandler.HandleGitHubRedirect)
   187Ôćĺ	mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github/callback", oauthHandler.HandleGitHubCallback)
   188Ôćĺ	mux.Handle("GET /auth/me", sessionMw(http.HandlerFunc(oauthHandler.HandleMe)))
   189Ôćĺ	mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/logout", oauthHandler.HandleLogout)
   190Ôćĺ}

1Ôćĺ# Architecture: Changelogue
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Overview
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺChangelogue is an event-driven, hybrid-architecture system designed to centralize release discovery, automate validation, manage configurations, and distribute targeted notifications. It combines the high-concurrency performance of a Go-based polling engine with the reasoning capabilities of LLM-based SRE agents. By leveraging PostgreSQL for both persistent storage and message brokering, the system maintains high reliability and transactional consistency within a streamlined, single-binary deployment.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ* **Backend / Engine:** Go (Golang). Chosen for its lightweight concurrency model (Goroutines) to handle simultaneous polling across multiple registries.
    10Ôćĺ* **Frontend / Dashboard:** Next.js (React) with Tailwind CSS. Provides a fast, modern control center for viewing release streams and managing configurations.
    11Ôćĺ* **Database, Queue, & Event Bus:** PostgreSQL.
    12Ôćĺ* *Persistent Storage:* Standard relational tables for release metadata and system configurations.
    13Ôćĺ* *Task Queue:* Utilizes `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (via the **River** Go library) for robust, concurrent background job processing without race conditions.
    14Ôćĺ* *Pub/Sub:* Utilizes native `LISTEN` and `NOTIFY` for real-time broadcasting to connected clients.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / OpenAI-compatible) orchestrated via [ADK-Go](https://google.github.io/adk-go/) v0.5.0 for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous release evaluation.
    18Ôćĺ* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ## 2. System Design & Abstractions
    21Ôćĺ
    22ÔćĺThe system is decoupled into four primary layers communicating entirely through PostgreSQL using the Transactional Outbox pattern:
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ1. **Ingestion Layer** ÔÇö Polling workers and webhook handlers discover new releases from upstream registries.
    25Ôćĺ2. **Notification Routing** ÔÇö River workers send immediate notifications to source-level subscribers on new releases.
    26Ôćĺ3. **Agent Layer** ÔÇö ADK-Go agents research releases, consult context sources, and produce semantic release reports.
    27Ôćĺ4. **Routing & Output** ÔÇö Notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks) deliver alerts via `Sender`.
    28Ôćĺ
    29ÔćĺA **Project** is the central domain entity ÔÇö it groups multiple ingestion sources, context sources, and notification subscriptions under a single tracked piece of software.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ```mermaid
    32Ôćĺgraph LR
    33Ôćĺ    %% Data Flow
    34Ôćĺ    subgraph Dashboard [API & Dashboard]
    35Ôćĺ        API[REST API] --> |CRUD| PG
    36Ôćĺ        API --> |SSE| Browser[Next.js Dashboard]
    37Ôćĺ    end
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ    subgraph Ingestion [Ingestion Layer]
    40Ôćĺ        I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    41Ôćĺ        I_Git(GitHub Atom Feed) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    42Ôćĺ    end
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ    subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]
    45Ôćĺ        Engine((Go Engine)) --> |INSERT release + jobs\nTransactional Outbox| PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    46Ôćĺ    end
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ    subgraph Notification [Notification Routing]
    49Ôćĺ        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver notify_release| NotifyWorker[Notify Worker]
    50Ôćĺ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Slack(Slack)
    51Ôćĺ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Discord(Discord)
    52Ôćĺ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Email(Email)
    53Ôćĺ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Hook(Webhooks)
    54Ôćĺ    end
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ    subgraph AgentLayer [Agent Layer]
    57Ôćĺ        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver agent_run| AgentWorker[Agent Worker]
    58Ôćĺ        AgentWorker --> |ADK-Go| LLM[LLM Provider]
    59Ôćĺ        AgentWorker --> |Research| ContextSources[Context Sources]
    60Ôćĺ        AgentWorker --> |Produce| SemanticRelease[Semantic Release + Report]
    61Ôćĺ    end
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ```
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ### Entity Relationship Model
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ```mermaid
    68ÔćĺerDiagram
    69Ôćĺ    projects ||--o{ sources : "has many"
    70Ôćĺ    projects ||--o{ context_sources : "has many"
    71Ôćĺ    projects ||--o{ semantic_releases : "has many"
    72Ôćĺ    projects ||--o{ agent_runs : "has many"
    73Ôćĺ    sources ||--o{ releases : "produces"
    74Ôćĺ    subscriptions }o--|| notification_channels : "routes to"
    75Ôćĺ    subscriptions }o--o| sources : "source-level"
    76Ôćĺ    subscriptions }o--o| projects : "project-level"
    77Ôćĺ    semantic_releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "composed of"
    78Ôćĺ    releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "contributes to"
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ    projects {
    81Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
    82Ôćĺ        string name UK
    83Ôćĺ        text agent_prompt
    84Ôćĺ        jsonb agent_rules
    85Ôćĺ    }
    86Ôćĺ    sources {
    87Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
    88Ôćĺ        uuid project_id FK
    89Ôćĺ        string provider
    90Ôćĺ        string repository UK
    91Ôćĺ        boolean enabled
    92Ôćĺ    }
    93Ôćĺ    releases {
    94Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
    95Ôćĺ        uuid source_id FK
    96Ôćĺ        string version
    97Ôćĺ        jsonb raw_data
    98Ôćĺ    }
    99Ôćĺ    semantic_releases {
   100Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
   101Ôćĺ        uuid project_id FK
   102Ôćĺ        string version
   103Ôćĺ        jsonb report
   104Ôćĺ        string status
   105Ôćĺ    }
   106Ôćĺ    agent_runs {
   107Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
   108Ôćĺ        uuid project_id FK
   109Ôćĺ        uuid semantic_release_id FK
   110Ôćĺ        string trigger
   111Ôćĺ        string status
   112Ôćĺ    }
   113Ôćĺ    subscriptions {
   114Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
   115Ôćĺ        uuid channel_id FK
   116Ôćĺ        string type
   117Ôćĺ        uuid source_id FK
   118Ôćĺ        uuid project_id FK
   119Ôćĺ    }
   120Ôćĺ    notification_channels {
   121Ôćĺ        uuid id PK
   122Ôćĺ        string type
   123Ôćĺ        string name
   124Ôćĺ        jsonb config
   125Ôćĺ    }
   126Ôćĺ```
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### 2.1 The Event-Driven Backbone (PostgreSQL Only)
   129Ôćĺ
   130ÔćĺComponents do not call each other synchronously. Instead, they rely on PostgreSQL to guarantee delivery:
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ* **The Transactional Outbox:** When a new release is detected, the ingestion worker writes the metadata to the `releases` table (linked to its `source_id`) and simultaneously enqueues River jobs (notification and/or agent) *within the exact same SQL transaction*. This guarantees no events are ever lost.
   133Ôćĺ* **Real-time Pub/Sub:** Database triggers use `pg_notify` to broadcast lightweight events (e.g., telling the Next.js UI via SSE to refresh) over standard Postgres connections using `LISTEN`.
   134Ôćĺ* **Reliable Queues:** River workers process jobs using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, ensuring exactly-once processing for both notification delivery and agent runs.
   135Ôćĺ* **Project-Centric Model:** All data flows through the `projects` Ôćĺ `sources` Ôćĺ `releases` hierarchy. Subscriptions can attach at the source level (for raw release notifications) or the project level (for semantic release notifications).
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ### 2.2 Provider Interfaces (I/O)
   138Ôćĺ
   139ÔćĺAll external integrations are abstracted behind strict Go interfaces.
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ* `IIngestionSource`: Standardizes how polling workers fetch data. Adding a new registry (like npm or NuGet) only requires implementing this interface. Each implementation maps to a `source_type` in the database. Current implementations: Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI.
   142Ôćĺ* `Sender`: Standardizes output routing. Each implementation maps to a `type` in the `notification_channels` table (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ### 2.3 REST API & Dashboard
   145Ôćĺ
   146ÔćĺThe Go server exposes a RESTful API (`/api/v1`) serving the Next.js dashboard and external consumers:
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ* **Resource CRUD:** Projects, sources, subscriptions, notification channels, context sources ÔÇö all manageable through the API.
   149Ôćĺ* **Read-Only Releases:** Releases and semantic releases are queryable but not directly writable via the API ÔÇö they're created exclusively through the ingestion layer and agent runs.
   150Ôćĺ* **Source Operations:** Manual poll trigger (`POST /sources/{id}/poll`) and channel test notifications (`POST /channels/{id}/test`).
   151Ôćĺ* **SSE Real-Time Events:** `GET /api/v1/events` streams server-sent events backed by PostgreSQL `LISTEN/NOTIFY`, pushing release and semantic release updates to connected dashboard clients.
   152Ôćĺ* **API Key Auth:** Bearer token authentication with hashed key storage. `NO_AUTH=true` disables auth for development.
   153Ôćĺ* **Rate Limiting:** Per-key token bucket (10 rps, 20 burst) with standard `X-Ratelimit-*` response headers.
   154Ôćĺ* **Stats & Trends:** Dashboard stats and time-bucketed release count trends via `/stats` and `/stats/trend`.
   155Ôćĺ
   156ÔćĺSee [API.md](API.md) for the full endpoint reference.
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ### 2.4 Notification Routing
   159Ôćĺ
   160ÔćĺWhen a new source release is detected, a `notify_release` River job is enqueued in the same transaction as the release insert. The notification worker:
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ1. Resolves all source-level subscriptions for the release's source.
   163Ôćĺ2. For each subscription, sends a notification to the linked channel via `Sender`.
   164Ôćĺ3. Checks the project's agent rules ÔÇö if the release matches (major bump, security patch, version pattern), enqueues an `agent_run` job automatically.
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ### 2.5 Agent Layer (Implemented)
   167Ôćĺ
   168ÔćĺFor project-level intelligence, an `agent_run` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go v0.5.0) that:
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ1. Gathers recent source releases for the project using the `get_releases` tool.
   171Ôćĺ2. Inspects individual release details (changelogs, raw data) using `get_release_detail`.
   172Ôćĺ3. Consults context sources (runbooks, docs, monitoring dashboards) using `list_context_sources`.
   173Ôćĺ4. Produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport` (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, download commands, urgency, recommendation).
   174Ôćĺ
   175ÔćĺAgent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` (custom instructions) and `agent_rules` (structured triggers like `on_major_release`, `on_security_patch`, `version_pattern`, `wait_for_all_sources`). Agent runs are tracked in the `agent_runs` table for observability and auditability.
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ**LLM Provider Support:**
   178Ôćĺ
   179ÔćĺThe agent supports multiple LLM backends via the `LLM_PROVIDER` environment variable:
   180Ôćĺ* **Gemini** (default) ÔÇö Uses `GOOGLE_API_KEY` with ADK-Go's native Gemini model integration.
   181Ôćĺ* **OpenAI** ÔÇö Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `OPENAI_BASE_URL` via an OpenAI-compatible adapter (`internal/agent/openai/`).
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ**Implementation details (`internal/agent/`):**
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ* **`tools.go`** ÔÇö Three ADK function tools (`get_releases`, `get_release_detail`, `list_context_sources`) created via `functiontool.New()`. Tools are scoped to a project via `toolFactory` which holds the `AgentDataStore` and project ID.
   186Ôćĺ* **`orchestrator.go`** ÔÇö `Orchestrator` manages the full agent lifecycle: loading project config, creating an ADK `llmagent` with the configured LLM backend, running the agent via `runner.Runner`, parsing the JSON report, and persisting the `SemanticRelease` in a transaction.
   187Ôćĺ* **`worker.go`** ÔÇö `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`, loading the agent run from the store and delegating to `Orchestrator.RunAgent()`.
   188Ôćĺ* **Graceful degradation** ÔÇö If no LLM API key is set, the orchestrator is not created, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system continues to function. Agent jobs remain in the River queue until the key is configured.
   189Ôćĺ
   190Ôćĺ### 2.6 Agentic Tooling (Planned: SRE Validation)
   191Ôćĺ
   192ÔćĺFor deep validation, Changelogue will utilize SRE agents with a suite of abstracted tools:
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺ* `UpgradeBaseABoxConfig(version)`
   195Ôćĺ* `CheckAgentStatus(environment)`
   196Ôćĺ* `SyncOpsOpsack(payload)`
   197ÔćĺThis will allow the agent to autonomously deploy a sandbox, verify that the deployment is healthy, and rollback or alert on failure.
   198Ôćĺ
   199Ôćĺ## 3. Data Flow: Lifecycle of a Release Event
   200Ôćĺ
   201Ôćĺ1. **Discovery:** A Go worker polling Docker Hub detects a new base image tag for a configured source (e.g., the "Docker Hub" source under the "Go Runtime" project). Source-level exclusion filters (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`) are applied ÔÇö filtered versions are discarded immediately.
   202Ôćĺ2. **Ingestion & Transaction:** The worker stores the raw upstream payload and executes a single database transaction to insert the record into `releases` (linked to its `source_id`) and enqueue a `notify_release` River job.
   203Ôćĺ3. **Notification (Source-Level):** A River worker picks up the `notify_release` job, resolves source-level subscriptions, and sends alerts to configured notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   204Ôćĺ4. **Agent Rule Check:** The same notification worker evaluates the project's `agent_rules` against the new release version. If criteria match (major bump, security patch, version pattern), it enqueues an `agent_run` River job.
   205Ôćĺ5. **Agent Analysis (Project-Level):** A River worker picks up the `agent_run` job. The ADK-Go agent gathers recent releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured report (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, urgency, recommendation).
   206Ôćĺ6. **Broadcast:** PostgreSQL triggers fire `NOTIFY` payloads on release insert and semantic release completion, pushing SSE events to connected dashboard clients.
   207Ôćĺ7. **Project-Level Notification:** When a semantic release is completed, project-level subscribers are notified with the AI-generated report.
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ## 4. Directory Structure
   210Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺ```text
   212Ôćĺ/changelogue
   213ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ cmd/
   214ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ server/          # Main Go application entry point
   215ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ agent/           # Agent CLI ÔÇö run agent analysis for a project
   216ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ internal/
   217ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ api/             # REST API handlers, middleware, SSE broadcaster
   218ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ ingestion/       # Polling workers (IIngestionSource: Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI)
   219ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ agent/           # ADK-Go agent for semantic release analysis
   220ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ openai/      # OpenAI-compatible LLM provider adapter
   221ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ routing/         # Notification routing worker and channel implementations (Sender)
   222ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ queue/           # River queue job definitions (NotifyJobArgs, AgentJobArgs)
   223ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ db/              # Connection pool and schema migrations
   224ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ models/          # Shared domain structs (Release, Project, SemanticRelease, etc.)
   225ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ web/                 # Next.js frontend application
   226ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ app/             # App Router pages (dashboard, projects, releases, channels, etc.)
   227ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ components/      # React components organized by domain
   228ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ lib/             # Client utilities and API wrappers
   229ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ scripts/             # Integration test harness
   230ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/                # Design documents and plans
   231ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ go.mod
   232Ôćĺ```

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

   122Ôćĺ  delete: (id: string) =>
   123Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   124Ôćĺ};
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ// --- Semantic Releases ---
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺexport const semanticReleases = {
   129Ôćĺ  listAll: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   130Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   131Ôćĺ  list: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   132Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   133Ôćĺ  get: (id: string) =>
   134Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`),
   135Ôćĺ  delete: (id: string) =>
   136Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   137Ôćĺ  getSources: (id: string) =>
   138Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}/sources`),
   139Ôćĺ};
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ// --- Agent ---
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺexport const agent = {
   144Ôćĺ  triggerRun: (projectId: string, version?: string) =>
   145Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/run`, {
   146Ôćĺ      method: "POST",
   147Ôćĺ      body: JSON.stringify({ trigger: "test", version: version || undefined }),
   148Ôćĺ    }),

   150Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/runs?page=${page}`),
   151Ôćĺ  getRun: (id: string) =>

   154Ôćĺ

   160Ôćĺ  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`),
   161Ôćĺ  create: (input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   162Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>("/subscriptions", {
   163Ôćĺ      method: "POST",

   178Ôćĺ  batchDelete: (input: BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput) =>
   179Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<null>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   180Ôćĺ      method: "DELETE",
   181Ôćĺ      body: JSON.stringify(input),

   184Ôćĺ

   210Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺexport const todos = {
   212Ôćĺ  list: (status?: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, aggregated = false) => {

   221Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/acknowledge${cascade ? '?cascade=true' : ''}`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   222Ôćĺ  resolve: (id: string, cascade = false) =>
   223Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/resolve${cascade ? '?cascade=true' : ''}`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   224Ôćĺ  reopen: (id: string) =>
   225Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/reopen`, { method: "PATCH" }),

   227Ôćĺ
   228Ôćĺ// --- System ---
   229Ôćĺ
   230Ôćĺexport const system = {
   231Ôćĺ  health: () => request<HealthStatus>("/health"),
   232Ôćĺ  stats: () => request<ApiResponse<Stats>>("/stats"),
   233Ôćĺ  trend: (granularity: "daily" | "weekly" | "monthly" = "daily", days = 7) =>
   234Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<TrendData>>(`/stats/trend?granularity=${granularity}&days=${days}`),
   235Ôćĺ};
   236Ôćĺ
   237Ôćĺ// --- Discovery ---
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺexport const discover = {
   240Ôćĺ  github: (params?: { q?: string; language?: string }) => {
   241Ôćĺ    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   242Ôćĺ    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   243Ôćĺ    if (params?.language) search.set("language", params.language);
   244Ôćĺ    const qs = search.toString();
   245Ôćĺ    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/github${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   246Ôćĺ  },
   247Ôćĺ  dockerhub: (params?: { q?: string }) => {
   248Ôćĺ    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   249Ôćĺ    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   250Ôćĺ    const qs = search.toString();
   251Ôćĺ    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/dockerhub${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   252Ôćĺ  },
   253Ôćĺ};
   254Ôćĺ
   255Ôćĺ// --- Onboarding ---
   256Ôćĺ
   257Ôćĺexport const onboard = {
   258Ôćĺ  scan: async (repoUrl: string): Promise<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>> => {
   259Ôćĺ    const res = await fetch(`${BASE}/onboard/scan`, {
   260Ôćĺ      method: "POST",
   261Ôćĺ      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
   262Ôćĺ      body: JSON.stringify({ repo_url: repoUrl }),
   263Ôćĺ    });
   264Ôćĺ    // 409 returns the existing active scan ÔÇö treat as success
   265Ôćĺ    if (res.status === 409) {
   266Ôćĺ      return res.json();
   267Ôćĺ    }
   268Ôćĺ    if (!res.ok) {
   269Ôćĺ      const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
   270Ôćĺ      throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
   271Ôćĺ    }
   272Ôćĺ    return res.json();
   273Ôćĺ  },
   274Ôćĺ  getScan: (id: string) =>
   275Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}`),
   276Ôćĺ  apply: (id: string, selections: OnboardSelection[]) =>
   277Ôćĺ    request<ApiResponse<OnboardApplyResult>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}/apply`, {
   278Ôćĺ      method: "POST",
   279Ôćĺ      body: JSON.stringify({ selections }),
   280Ôćĺ    }),
   281Ôćĺ};
   282Ôćĺ

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase and the spec. Let me compile the review.

---

## **Issues Found**

### Critical Issues

**1. NO_AUTH / dev mode makes the suggestions endpoints non-functional.**

In `NO_AUTH` mode, the `chain` middleware is `publicChain` (no auth), meaning `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` returns `nil` -- there is no user in context. The spec says the new endpoints use "Auth required (session cookie)" and will extract `github_login` from the session user, but it does not address how this works in `NO_AUTH` mode. The existing `Auth` middleware is entirely skipped when `deps.NoAuth` is true (see `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go` line 42-45). The dev user has `GitHubLogin: "dev"`, and calling `https://api.github.com/users/dev/starred` would return some random GitHub user's stars, not the developer's.

**Recommendation**: The spec must explicitly state how the suggestions routes behave in `NO_AUTH` mode. Options: (a) register them on `chain` (auth-required) even in `NO_AUTH` mode -- but then they fail since `chain == publicChain`; (b) add special-case handling in the handler to check if user is nil or login is "dev" and return an empty result or an error; (c) use the `DevSession` middleware wrapper for these specific routes in `NO_AUTH` mode. This needs to be specified.

**2. The `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` endpoint relies on starred repos being public, but the GitHub API endpoint `GET /users/{username}/starred` only returns *public* stars -- this is correct for a server-side token, but this limitation is not documented.**

The spec says "Public GitHub API" and "no per-user token storage", which means the server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` authenticates the request for rate-limit purposes, but since it is not the user's own token, only the user's publicly-visible stars are returned. If a user has starred private repos, those will not appear. This is probably fine but should be explicitly called out as a known limitation.

**3. The `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint uses `GET /users/{github_login}/repos` which only returns the user's *public* repositories.**

Same issue as above. The `GET /users/{username}/repos` endpoint only lists public repos when authenticated with a different user's token. The spec should document this explicitly. More importantly: for the dependency scanning use case, users likely want to scan their *private* repos too (that is where most real dependencies live). The spec should acknowledge this limitation clearly in the "Non-goals" section or "Edge Cases."

**4. The spec says "Filters out repos already tracked as sources" for the stars endpoint but does not specify the mechanism.**

The handler needs access to the `SourcesStore` to query which repositories are already tracked. The spec lists `internal/api/suggestions.go` as the new file with "two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching" but does not mention that the handler needs a `SourcesStore` dependency. Looking at how the `DiscoverHandler` is instantiated in `server.go` (line 134: `discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")`), the suggestions handler will need more dependencies.

**Recommendation**: The spec should state that `NewSuggestionsHandler` takes `deps.HTTPClient`, `deps.SourcesStore`, and optionally a `GITHUB_TOKEN` string.

### Moderate Issues

**5. Missing specification of how to extract `github_login` from the request context in the handler.**

The spec says "Auth required (session cookie)" but does not specify that the handler calls `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` to get the `*auth.User` and reads `user.GitHubLogin`. This is an implementation detail, but since the spec enumerates the exact GitHub API URLs with `{github_login}`, it should state where that value comes from in the handler. More critically, when the request comes via Bearer token (API key auth), there is **no user in context** at all -- the `Auth` middleware (line 36 in `auth.go`) calls `next.ServeHTTP(w, r)` without injecting a user. API key users cannot use these endpoints. The spec should note this.

**6. The response format for `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` does not wrap in the standard `ApiResponse` envelope.**

Looking at the existing API client (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`), all responses use `ApiResponse<T>` which wraps data in `{ data: T }`. The spec's response description says "array of `{ name, full_name, ... }`" but doesn't clarify whether it follows the standard envelope pattern. The frontend client modifications listed (`suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()`) need to know the response shape.

**Recommendation**: Explicitly state the response uses `ApiResponse<[]DiscoverItem>` format consistent with other endpoints, or define a new response type.

**7. The spec says "Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark" in StarsTab but the filtering happens both server-side ("Filters out repos already tracked as sources") and client-side ("Already-tracked repos dimmed").**

These are contradictory. If the server filters them out, the client will never see them to dim. If the intent is to dim rather than hide, the server should return them with a `tracked: true` flag instead of filtering. The spec needs to pick one approach.

**8. Missing pagination details for the `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint.**

The stars endpoint says "Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)" which implies the server fetches multiple pages from GitHub and aggregates. The repos endpoint just says `per_page=100` in the URL. The spec should clarify: does the server do multi-page fetching for repos too? GitHub caps `per_page` at 100, so users with more than 100 repos will see a truncated list. Is that acceptable? Should there be cursor-based pagination on the Changelogue API side?

**9. Cache invalidation not addressed.**

The spec says "Caches per-user for 1 hour" for both endpoints. But if a user tracks a repo from the stars list, the cached results will still show that repo for up to an hour (even if server-side filtering is applied, the cache would need to be invalidated or the filtering must happen post-cache). The spec should clarify whether filtering happens before or after the cache, or whether tracking a repo invalidates the cache.

### Minor Issues

**10. The spec says the frontend falls back to `DiscoverySection` for "unauthenticated users" but the auth context in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/auth/context.tsx` redirects unauthenticated users to `/login` (line 65).** 

If unauthenticated users are always redirected to `/login`, they will never see the dashboard page at all. The "unauthenticated fallback" scenario only applies in `NO_AUTH` dev mode (where `user` is always set). The spec's framing about "unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel" is misleading given the actual auth flow.

**Recommendation**: Reframe as "Users without a GitHub login (e.g., API key auth or dev mode) see the existing trending carousel."

**11. The "Files to Create/Modify" section does not mention adding `GITHUB_TOKEN` to the `SuggestionsHandler` initialization.**

The discover handler currently does not use `GITHUB_TOKEN`. The ingestion layer and onboard scanner read it from `os.Getenv` directly. The spec should clarify whether the suggestions handler also reads from env directly (like the onboard scanner) or receives it as a constructor parameter (more testable).

**12. No error response format specified.**

The spec says "Show friendly error if rate limited" for GitHub rate limiting but does not specify the HTTP status code or error response body the Changelogue API should return. Looking at existing patterns in `discover.go`, the convention is `RespondError(w, r, http.StatusBadGateway, "upstream_error", ...)`. The spec should match this.

**13. The spec does not mention tests.**

No test file is listed in the "Files to Create/Modify" section. Given the existing `discover_test.go` likely exists and the pattern of thorough testing in the codebase, the spec should call out `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` as a new file.

---

**Verdict: Issues Found** -- 13 issues identified. Issues 1, 4, 5, and 7 are the most important to resolve before implementation, as they would cause confusion or bugs during development. The rest are specification gaps that a senior implementer could figure out from context but would benefit from explicit documentation.

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase and the spec. Let me compile the review.

---

## **Issues Found**

### Critical Issues

**1. NO_AUTH / dev mode makes the suggestions endpoints non-functional.**

In `NO_AUTH` mode, the `chain` middleware is `publicChain` (no auth), meaning `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` returns `nil` -- there is no user in context. The spec says the new endpoints use "Auth required (session cookie)" and will extract `github_login` from the session user, but it does not address how this works in `NO_AUTH` mode. The existing `Auth` middleware is entirely skipped when `deps.NoAuth` is true (see `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go` line 42-45). The dev user has `GitHubLogin: "dev"`, and calling `https://api.github.com/users/dev/starred` would return some random GitHub user's stars, not the developer's.

**Recommendation**: The spec must explicitly state how the suggestions routes behave in `NO_AUTH` mode. Options: (a) register them on `chain` (auth-required) even in `NO_AUTH` mode -- but then they fail since `chain == publicChain`; (b) add special-case handling in the handler to check if user is nil or login is "dev" and return an empty result or an error; (c) use the `DevSession` middleware wrapper for these specific routes in `NO_AUTH` mode. This needs to be specified.

**2. The `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` endpoint relies on starred repos being public, but the GitHub API endpoint `GET /users/{username}/starred` only returns *public* stars -- this is correct for a server-side token, but this limitation is not documented.**

The spec says "Public GitHub API" and "no per-user token storage", which means the server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` authenticates the request for rate-limit purposes, but since it is not the user's own token, only the user's publicly-visible stars are returned. If a user has starred private repos, those will not appear. This is probably fine but should be explicitly called out as a known limitation.

**3. The `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint uses `GET /users/{github_login}/repos` which only returns the user's *public* repositories.**

Same issue as above. The `GET /users/{username}/repos` endpoint only lists public repos when authenticated with a different user's token. The spec should document this explicitly. More importantly: for the dependency scanning use case, users likely want to scan their *private* repos too (that is where most real dependencies live). The spec should acknowledge this limitation clearly in the "Non-goals" section or "Edge Cases."

**4. The spec says "Filters out repos already tracked as sources" for the stars endpoint but does not specify the mechanism.**

The handler needs access to the `SourcesStore` to query which repositories are already tracked. The spec lists `internal/api/suggestions.go` as the new file with "two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching" but does not mention that the handler needs a `SourcesStore` dependency. Looking at how the `DiscoverHandler` is instantiated in `server.go` (line 134: `discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")`), the suggestions handler will need more dependencies.

**Recommendation**: The spec should state that `NewSuggestionsHandler` takes `deps.HTTPClient`, `deps.SourcesStore`, and optionally a `GITHUB_TOKEN` string.

### Moderate Issues

**5. Missing specification of how to extract `github_login` from the request context in the handler.**

The spec says "Auth required (session cookie)" but does not specify that the handler calls `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` to get the `*auth.User` and reads `user.GitHubLogin`. This is an implementation detail, but since the spec enumerates the exact GitHub API URLs with `{github_login}`, it should state where that value comes from in the handler. More critically, when the request comes via Bearer token (API key auth), there is **no user in context** at all -- the `Auth` middleware (line 36 in `auth.go`) calls `next.ServeHTTP(w, r)` without injecting a user. API key users cannot use these endpoints. The spec should note this.

**6. The response format for `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` does not wrap in the standard `ApiResponse` envelope.**

Looking at the existing API client (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`), all responses use `ApiResponse<T>` which wraps data in `{ data: T }`. The spec's response description says "array of `{ name, full_name, ... }`" but doesn't clarify whether it follows the standard envelope pattern. The frontend client modifications listed (`suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()`) need to know the response shape.

**Recommendation**: Explicitly state the response uses `ApiResponse<[]DiscoverItem>` format consistent with other endpoints, or define a new response type.

**7. The spec says "Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark" in StarsTab but the filtering happens both server-side ("Filters out repos already tracked as sources") and client-side ("Already-tracked repos dimmed").**

These are contradictory. If the server filters them out, the client will never see them to dim. If the intent is to dim rather than hide, the server should return them with a `tracked: true` flag instead of filtering. The spec needs to pick one approach.

**8. Missing pagination details for the `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint.**

The stars endpoint says "Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)" which implies the server fetches multiple pages from GitHub and aggregates. The repos endpoint just says `per_page=100` in the URL. The spec should clarify: does the server do multi-page fetching for repos too? GitHub caps `per_page` at 100, so users with more than 100 repos will see a truncated list. Is that acceptable? Should there be cursor-based pagination on the Changelogue API side?

**9. Cache invalidation not addressed.**

The spec says "Caches per-user for 1 hour" for both endpoints. But if a user tracks a repo from the stars list, the cached results will still show that repo for up to an hour (even if server-side filtering is applied, the cache would need to be invalidated or the filtering must happen post-cache). The spec should clarify whether filtering happens before or after the cache, or whether tracking a repo invalidates the cache.

### Minor Issues

**10. The spec says the frontend falls back to `DiscoverySection` for "unauthenticated users" but the auth context in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/auth/context.tsx` redirects unauthenticated users to `/login` (line 65).** 

If unauthenticated users are always redirected to `/login`, they will never see the dashboard page at all. The "unauthenticated fallback" scenario only applies in `NO_AUTH` dev mode (where `user` is always set). The spec's framing about "unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel" is misleading given the actual auth flow.

**Recommendation**: Reframe as "Users without a GitHub login (e.g., API key auth or dev mode) see the existing trending carousel."

**11. The "Files to Create/Modify" section does not mention adding `GITHUB_TOKEN` to the `SuggestionsHandler` initialization.**

The discover handler currently does not use `GITHUB_TOKEN`. The ingestion layer and onboard scanner read it from `os.Getenv` directly. The spec should clarify whether the suggestions handler also reads from env directly (like the onboard scanner) or receives it as a constructor parameter (more testable).

**12. No error response format specified.**

The spec says "Show friendly error if rate limited" for GitHub rate limiting but does not specify the HTTP status code or error response body the Changelogue API should return. Looking at existing patterns in `discover.go`, the convention is `RespondError(w, r, http.StatusBadGateway, "upstream_error", ...)`. The spec should match this.

**13. The spec does not mention tests.**

No test file is listed in the "Files to Create/Modify" section. Given the existing `discover_test.go` likely exists and the pattern of thorough testing in the codebase, the spec should call out `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` as a new file.

---

**Verdict: Issues Found** -- 13 issues identified. Issues 1, 4, 5, and 7 are the most important to resolve before implementation, as they would cause confusion or bugs during development. The rest are specification gaps that a senior implementer could figure out from context but would benefit from explicit documentation.
Good review feedback. Let me fix the key issues in the spec.

# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ÔÇö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ÔÇö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ÔÇö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ÔÇö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist Ôćĺ scan selected Ôćĺ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Backend API

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)
- Caches per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`)
- Filters out repos already tracked as sources
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ÔÇö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ÔÇö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ÔÇö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ÔÇö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for authenticated users. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` for unauthenticated users.

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button Ôćĺ fetches repo list Ôćĺ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc Ôćĺ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp Ôćĺ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Show friendly error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Both tabs filter/dim repos and dependencies already tracked as sources.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ÔÇö two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ÔÇö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ÔÇö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ÔÇö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

### Modified files
- `internal/api/server.go` ÔÇö register new `/api/v1/suggestions/` routes
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` ÔÇö add `suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()` client methods
- `web/app/page.tsx` ÔÇö conditionally render `SuggestionsSection` vs `DiscoverySection` based on auth state

## Non-goals (future work)

- Private repo access (requires storing per-user OAuth tokens, expanded scopes)
- Auto-fetch on first login
- Recommendation ranking / ML-based suggestions
- Non-GitHub providers (GitLab, Bitbucket stars/repos)

input.content:
# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ÔÇö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ÔÇö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ÔÇö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ÔÇö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist Ôćĺ scan selected Ôćĺ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Known Limitations

- **Public data only**: `GET /users/{username}/starred` and `GET /users/{username}/repos` with a server-side token only return publicly visible stars and public repos. Private stars and private repos are not accessible. This is by design for the current scope ÔÇö private repo support is a future goal requiring per-user token storage.
- **Repo cap**: GitHub API caps `per_page` at 100. Users with 100+ repos see only the most recently pushed 100. Acceptable for now.

## Backend API

### Handler Construction

`NewSuggestionsHandler(httpClient *http.Client, sourcesStore db.SourcesStore, githubToken string)` ÔÇö reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` from env at initialization (same pattern as onboard scanner). Needs `SourcesStore` dependency to check which repos are already tracked.

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required: extracts user via `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())`, reads `user.GitHubLogin`
- In `NO_AUTH` mode or when `github_login` is empty/`"dev"`: returns empty array (not an error)
- API key auth (Bearer token): returns 403 ÔÇö these endpoints require a session with a GitHub identity
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page, fetch up to 4 pages)
- Caches raw GitHub response per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`). Tracked-repo filtering applied post-cache so tracking a repo is reflected immediately without cache invalidation.
- Returns already-tracked repos with a `tracked: true` flag (not filtered out) so the frontend can dim them with a checkmark
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]SuggestionItem>` where `SuggestionItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github", tracked: bool }`
- Rate limit error: returns `502 Bad Gateway` with `{ error: "upstream_error", message: "GitHub API rate limit exceeded, try again later" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Same auth requirements as `/stars`
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]RepoItem>` where `RepoItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ÔÇö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ÔÇö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ÔÇö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ÔÇö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for users with a `github_login`. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` when `github_login` is absent (dev mode, API key auth).

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark (using `tracked` flag from API)
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination (client-side, data already fetched)

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button Ôćĺ fetches repo list Ôćĺ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc Ôćĺ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp Ôćĺ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Return 502 with descriptive error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Stars tab returns `tracked: true` flag; frontend dims with checkmark. Deps tab results similarly indicate already-tracked dependencies.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.
- **NO_AUTH / dev mode**: Suggestions endpoints return empty arrays when `github_login` is empty or `"dev"`. Frontend shows trending carousel fallback.
- **Cache vs. tracking**: GitHub API response is cached; tracked-repo filtering is applied post-cache so it reflects immediately.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ÔÇö `SuggestionsHandler` with `handleStars`, `handleRepos` + GitHub API fetching + per-user caching
- `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` ÔÇö unit tests for handlers (mock GitHub API, mock SourcesStore)
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ÔÇö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ÔÇö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ÔÇö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

### Modified files
- `internal/api/server.go` ÔÇö register new `/api/v1/suggestions/` routes on auth-required chain, construct `SuggestionsHandler` with `deps.SourcesStore` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` ÔÇö add `suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()` client methods
- `web/app/page.tsx` ÔÇö conditionally render `SuggestionsSection` vs `DiscoverySection` based on `github_login` presence

## Non-goals (future work)

- Private repo/star access (requires storing per-user OAuth tokens, expanded scopes)
- Auto-fetch on first login
- Recommendation ranking / ML-based suggestions
- Non-GitHub providers (GitLab, Bitbucket stars/repos)

toolUseResult.content:
# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ÔÇö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ÔÇö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ÔÇö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ÔÇö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist Ôćĺ scan selected Ôćĺ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Known Limitations

- **Public data only**: `GET /users/{username}/starred` and `GET /users/{username}/repos` with a server-side token only return publicly visible stars and public repos. Private stars and private repos are not accessible. This is by design for the current scope ÔÇö private repo support is a future goal requiring per-user token storage.
- **Repo cap**: GitHub API caps `per_page` at 100. Users with 100+ repos see only the most recently pushed 100. Acceptable for now.

## Backend API

### Handler Construction

`NewSuggestionsHandler(httpClient *http.Client, sourcesStore db.SourcesStore, githubToken string)` ÔÇö reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` from env at initialization (same pattern as onboard scanner). Needs `SourcesStore` dependency to check which repos are already tracked.

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required: extracts user via `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())`, reads `user.GitHubLogin`
- In `NO_AUTH` mode or when `github_login` is empty/`"dev"`: returns empty array (not an error)
- API key auth (Bearer token): returns 403 ÔÇö these endpoints require a session with a GitHub identity
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page, fetch up to 4 pages)
- Caches raw GitHub response per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`). Tracked-repo filtering applied post-cache so tracking a repo is reflected immediately without cache invalidation.
- Returns already-tracked repos with a `tracked: true` flag (not filtered out) so the frontend can dim them with a checkmark
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]SuggestionItem>` where `SuggestionItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github", tracked: bool }`
- Rate limit error: returns `502 Bad Gateway` with `{ error: "upstream_error", message: "GitHub API rate limit exceeded, try again later" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Same auth requirements as `/stars`
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]RepoItem>` where `RepoItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ÔÇö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ÔÇö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ÔÇö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ÔÇö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for users with a `github_login`. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` when `github_login` is absent (dev mode, API key auth).

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark (using `tracked` flag from API)
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination (client-side, data already fetched)

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button Ôćĺ fetches repo list Ôćĺ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc Ôćĺ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp Ôćĺ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Return 502 with descriptive error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Stars tab returns `tracked: true` flag; frontend dims with checkmark. Deps tab results similarly indicate already-tracked dependencies.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.
- **NO_AUTH / dev mode**: Suggestions endpoints return empty arrays when `github_login` is empty or `"dev"`. Frontend shows trending carousel fallback.
- **Cache vs. tracking**: GitHub API response is cached; tracked-repo filtering is applied post-cache so it reflects immediately.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ÔÇö `SuggestionsHandler` with `handleStars`, `handleRepos` + GitHub API fetching + per-user caching
- `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` ÔÇö unit tests for handlers (mock GitHub API, mock SourcesStore)
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ÔÇö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ÔÇö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ÔÇö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

### Modified files
- `internal/api/server.go` ÔÇö register new `/api/v1/suggestions/` routes on auth-required chain, construct `SuggestionsHandler` with `deps.SourcesStore` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` ÔÇö add `suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()` client methods
- `web/app/page.tsx` ÔÇö conditionally render `SuggestionsSection` vs `DiscoverySection` based on `github_login` presence

## Non-goals (future work)

- Private repo/star access (requires storing per-user OAuth tokens, expanded scopes)
- Auto-fetch on first login
- Recommendation ranking / ML-based suggestions
- Non-GitHub providers (GitLab, Bitbucket stars/repos)

# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ÔÇö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ÔÇö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ÔÇö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ÔÇö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist Ôćĺ scan selected Ôćĺ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Backend API

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)
- Caches per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`)
- Filters out repos already tracked as sources
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ÔÇö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ÔÇö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ÔÇö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ÔÇö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for authenticated users. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` for unauthenticated users.

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button Ôćĺ fetches repo list Ôćĺ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc Ôćĺ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp Ôćĺ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Show friendly error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Both tabs filter/dim repos and dependencies already tracked as sources.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ÔÇö two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ÔÇö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ÔÇö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ÔÇö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

## Non-goals (future work)
